sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The player moved RIGHT again. The bbox shows [8,26] to [63,62], which is a large change. Let me check the player's new position. The animation changed a lot of cells (56), so the player might have moved significantly.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check new player position
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
blue_player = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'b' and n['pixels'] == 24]
print(f"Player position: {blue_player[0]['boundary']}")

# Check the area around the player
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nPlayer area (rows 7-16, cols 32-45):")
for i in range(7, 17):
    print(f"{i:2d}: {ascii_lines[i][32:45]}")

# Check if we're near the red path now
print("\nRed path area (rows 8-15, cols 36-44):")
for i in range(8, 16):
    print(f"{i:2d}: {ascii_lines[i][36:44]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

Let me try RIGHT again to move closer to the red path. The player seems to move in 6-column increments, but the next position would be cols 38-42, which would overlap with the red path.

- Start: [8,14]-[12,18]
- After 1st RIGHT: [8,20]-[12,24]
- After 2nd RIGHT: [8,26]-[12,30]

Let me try RIGHT to see what happens.

<parameter=code>
# Player at [8,32]-[12,36], red path at cols 39-41

</function>
